settings.tagline: A Sacred Abode of Serpent Divinity
settings.openingHours: Morning: 05:00 AM - 11:30 AM | Evening: 05:00 PM - 07:30 PM
settings.heroSubtitle: Experience the calm, sacred presence of serpent divinity amidst ancient Sarpa Kavu flora.
settings.address: Mevakkatu, Near Sacred Serpent Grove, Kerala 695001, India
settings.dressCode: Dhoti/Mundu for Gents (Shirts to be removed before entering the inner sanctum). Traditional Saree, Set Mundu, or Salwar for Ladies.
settings.parking: Ample parking available in the outer temple grounds for two-wheelers and light motor vehicles.
rules: Footwear must be deposited at the outer counter.
Maintain silence in the inner sanctum and Sarpa Kavu.
Electronic gadgets & photography prohibited near the deity and Sarpa Kavu.
settings.photographyPolicy: Photography allowed only in outer courtyard for personal non-commercial use. Strictly forbidden near Sarpa Kavu and Sanctum Sanctorum.

announcements.message: Annual Ayilyam Mahotsavam dates announced: Next month 14th to 16th. Special Noorum Palum online booking now open.

timings.name: Nirmalya Darshan & Abhishekam
timings.description: Opening of sanctum, holy bath of Nagadevatas with coconut water and sacred herbal water.

timings.name: Usha Pooja & Morning Archana
timings.description: Morning offerings accompanied by sacred chants and traditional Kerala temple percussion.

timings.name: Noorum Palum Offering at Sarpa Kavu
timings.description: Sacred serpent grove ritual offering turmeric, rice powder, tender coconut water, and cow milk.

timings.name: Uchha Pooja & Temple Closure
timings.description: Noon offering of Palpayasam and floral tributes followed by sanctum closure.

timings.name: Evening Reopening & Deeparadhana
timings.description: Illumination of hundreds of oil lamps around the temple shrine and divine oil lamp ceremony.

timings.name: Athazha Pooja & Night Closure
timings.description: Night prayers, floral pushpanjali, and closing of sanctum doors till next dawn.

events.title: Maha Ayilyam Mahotsavam
events.description: The most revered monthly festival dedicated to Shree Nagaraja and Nagayakshi. Features grand Noorum Palum, Nagachamayam, and Sarpa Pattu.

events.title: Sarpa Bali & Rahu Dosha Nivarana
events.description: A deeply spiritual evening ritual performed for pacifying Sarpa Dosham, Rahu-Ketu obstacles, and ensuring family harmony.

events.title: Vishu Kani & Serpent Blessing
events.description: Auspicious Kerala New Year celebrations with customary Vishukkani arrangement, Kani Kanal, and gold coin blessed prasadam.
schedule: 06:30 AM - Vishu Kaineettam Distribution by Chief Priest

poojas.name: Special Noorum Palum
poojas.description: Traditional sacred mix of milk, turmeric powder, tender coconut water, and rice flour offered at the idol of Nagaraja in Sarpa Kavu.

poojas.name: Maha Sarpa Bali Ritual
poojas.description: Elaborate multi-priest ritual with intricate powder mandala (Kalamezhuthu) designed for removing Nagadosham and health obstacles.

poojas.name: Rahu-Ketu Peeda Shanti Archana
poojas.description: Special floral pushpanjali with holy chantings focused on relieving astrological affliction of planetary nodes.

poojas.name: Nagachamayam & Manjal Abhishekam
poojas.description: Adorning Nagadevatas with pure sandalwood paste, floral garlands, and turmeric bath.

poojas.name: Palpayasam Offering
poojas.description: Rich milk and rice dessert prepared in temple kitchen with pure cow milk and ghee as divine offering.

poojas.name: Kadali Pazham Nivedyam & Archana
poojas.description: Offering of plantain fruits and red flowers to appease Nagaraja.

faqs.question: What is the significance of Noorum Palum offering at Mevakkatu?
faqs.answer: Noorum Palum is a sacred mixture of turmeric, rice powder, tender coconut water, and cow milk offered at the Sarpa Kavu to appease serpent deities. It is believed to cure skin ailments, bring peace to ancestral souls, and neutralize Rahu-Ketu doshas.

faqs.question: Are visitors required to follow a specific dress code?
faqs.answer: Yes. Men are requested to wear traditional Mundu/Dhoti without shirts inside the inner courtyard. Women should wear Sarees, Set Mundu, or traditional Salwar Kameez. Modern western attire is strictly discouraged.

faqs.question: How can remote devotees book poojas and receive receipts?
faqs.answer: Devotees can browse the Poojas section on this website, select the desired offering, provide their Birth Star (Nakshatra) and Devotee Name, complete the payment online, and instantly download a digital receipt with a unique transaction reference.

faqs.question: Can I make tax-deductible or recurring monthly donations?
faqs.answer: Yes. The temple donation portal supports one-time contributions as well as recurring monthly donations towards Sarpa Kavu ecological preservation, daily Annadanam, and temple restoration.

faqs.question: Is photography permitted inside the temple premises?
faqs.answer: Photography is strictly forbidden inside the inner sanctum and near the Sarpa Kavu to preserve spiritual tranquility. Photography in the outer periphery for personal memory is permitted.

messages.name: Sunitha Menon
messages.subject: Ayilyam Mahotsavam Accommodation Query
messages.message: Om Namah Shivaya. We plan to visit during the Ayilyam Mahotsavam next month. Could you inform us about nearby stay options?
